provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Central European alpine country.
EU member state since 1995.
Primary official language is German.
capital: Austria's most populous city.
Situated on the Danube river.
Historic centre of the Habsburg empire.
river: Flows for approximately 2,850 km.
Passes through or borders 10 countries.
Empties into the Black Sea.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Belgium is highly multilingual. Dutch and French official forms are presented together to reflect federal bilingual equality at the national level.

country: Western European country bordering the North Sea.
Federal state with three official languages (Dutch, French, German).
Founding member of the European Union.
capital: Houses the main institutions of the European Union.
Officially bilingual (Dutch and French).
Historic centre of Flemish textile trade.
river: Flows approximately 950 km through three countries.
Rises in France and reaches the North Sea via the Netherlands.
Major waterway for inland shipping in northwest Europe.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Central European country and EU member.
Most populous member state of the European Union.
Primary official language is German.
capital: Most populous city in the European Union.
Reunified after 1990 following decades of division.
Seat of the Federal Government and Bundestag.
river: Flows approximately 1,230 km through six countries.
Forms part of the French–German border in Alsace.
Empties into the North Sea via the Netherlands.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Country on the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe.
Constitutional monarchy and EU member state.
Primary official language is Spanish (Castilian).
capital: Largest city and political centre of Spain.
Located near the geographic centre of the Iberian Peninsula.
Home to the Royal Palace and the Prado Museum.
river: Longest river on the Iberian Peninsula (~1,038 km).
Rises in Spain and reaches the Atlantic at Lisbon.
Known in Portuguese as the Tejo.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Western European country.
Founding member of the European Union.
Borders the Atlantic and Mediterranean.
capital: France's most populous city.
Situated on the Seine river.
Major historic, cultural, and economic centre.
river: Longest river entirely within France (1,006 km).
Flows west into the Atlantic Ocean.
Valley is famous for its historic châteaux.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Southern European country shaped like a boot.
Founding member of the European Union.
Primary official language is Italian.
capital: Capital of Italy and historic seat of the Roman Empire.
Encloses the independent state of Vatican City.
Situated on the Tiber river.
river: Longest river entirely within Italy (~652 km).
Drains the northern Italian plain into the Adriatic.
Its delta is a protected UNESCO biosphere reserve.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Low-lying country in northwestern Europe.
Constitutional monarchy and EU member state.
Primary official language is Dutch.
capital: Constitutional capital of the Netherlands.
The Hague is the seat of government, not Amsterdam.
Known for its canal ring, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
river: Enters the Netherlands from Germany and splits into delta branches.
Known locally as the Rijn; the main branch is the Nederrijn.
Major commercial waterway linking the Ruhr to Rotterdam.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Chinese (Mandarin) is the official language; native form uses Han characters. Latin local_display uses standard Pinyin romanisation.

country: Most populous country in East Asia.
One of the world's oldest continuous civilisations.
Primary official language is Mandarin Chinese.
capital: Political centre of the People's Republic of China.
Home to the Forbidden City and Tiananmen Square.
Host of the 2008 and 2022 Olympic Games.
river: Longest river in Asia (~6,300 km).
Drains much of central China into the East China Sea.
Site of the Three Gorges Dam, one of the largest hydroelectric projects.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: India has 22 scheduled languages plus English; Hindi is the most widely spoken. Latin local_display uses standard Hindi romanisation.

country: Most populous country in South Asia.
Federal parliamentary republic with 28 states.
Hindi and English are the official languages of the union.
capital: Designated as India's capital in 1911, replacing Calcutta.
Part of the National Capital Territory of Delhi.
Seat of the Parliament, President, and Supreme Court.
river: Sacred to Hindus and central to Indian civilisation.
Flows approximately 2,525 km from the Himalayas to the Bay of Bengal.
Its basin is one of the most fertile and densely populated on Earth.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Japanese is the official language; native form uses kanji, hiragana, and katakana. Latin local_display uses standard Hepburn romanisation.

country: Island country in East Asia.
Comprises an archipelago of over 14,000 islands.
Highly mountainous and volcanic terrain.
capital: Most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Located on the eastern coast of Honshu.
Seat of the Japanese Emperor and government.
river: Longest river in Japan (367 km).
Flows through Nagano and Niigata prefectures.
Empties into the Sea of Japan.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Korean is the official language; native form uses Hangul. Latin local_display uses the Revised Romanisation of Korean.

country: Country occupying the southern half of the Korean Peninsula.
Presidential republic and major East Asian economy.
Primary official language is Korean, written in Hangul.
capital: Largest metropolitan area in South Korea.
Situated along the Han river, about 50 km from the DMZ.
Host of the 1988 Summer Olympics.
river: Major river of South Korea, flowing through Seoul.
Formed by the confluence of the Namhan and Bukhan.
Empties into the Yellow Sea at the border with North Korea.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Second-largest country in South America.
Federal republic of 23 provinces plus the capital district.
Primary official language is Spanish.
capital: Largest city and chief port of Argentina.
Located on the western shore of the Río de la Plata estuary.
Famous for tango, football, and European-style architecture.
river: Second-longest river in South America (~4,880 km).
Rises in Brazil and empties into the Río de la Plata.
Forms part of the Argentina–Paraguay border.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Largest country in South America.
Primary official language is Portuguese.
Contains the majority of the Amazon Rainforest.
capital: Purpose-built and inaugurated in 1960.
Located centrally in the Brazilian Highlands.
Known for its modernist architecture.
river: Largest river by discharge volume in the world.
Contains the largest global drainage basin.
Flows west to east into the Atlantic Ocean.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Federal republic in southern North America.
Most populous Spanish-speaking country in the world.
Primary official language is Spanish.
capital: Largest metropolitan area in North America.
Built on the site of the Aztec capital of Tenochtitlan.
Situated at ~2,240 m elevation in the Valley of Mexico.
river: Forms most of the border between Mexico and the United States.
Known in Mexico as the Río Bravo del Norte.
Flows approximately 3,050 km from Colorado to the Gulf of Mexico.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Federal republic of 50 states in North America.
Third-largest country by area and population.
Primary de facto language is English.
capital: Federal district not part of any U.S. state.
Seat of Congress, the White House, and the Supreme Court.
Named after the first U.S. president, George Washington.
river: Second-longest river in North America (~3,766 km).
Drains most of the continental United States.
Forms the border of ten U.S. states.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Arabic is the official language. Rendered in standard Latin romanisation per MVP conventions.

country: Transcontinental country in Northeast Africa.
Borders the Mediterranean and Red Seas.
Most populous country in the Arab world.
capital: Largest metropolitan area in the Middle East.
Situated on the Nile river.
Located near the Giza pyramid complex.
river: Often considered the world's longest river.
Flows north into the Mediterranean Sea.
Drainage basin spans 11 African countries.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: Kenya has two official languages (Swahili and English); both use Latin script.

country: East African country on the Indian Ocean.
Official languages are Swahili and English.
Contains the Great Rift Valley and Mount Kenya.
capital: Largest city in Kenya and major East African hub.
Location of the Kenya national parliament.
Name derives from a Maasai phrase meaning "cool water".
river: Longest river in Kenya (~1,000 km).
Flows from the Mount Kenya highlands to the Indian Ocean.
Supports extensive agricultural irrigation schemes.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: South Africa has 12 official languages. Afrikaans and Zulu are widely used alongside English. The most common alternative Latin-script forms are provided here as a representative baseline.

country: Southernmost country in Africa.
Has three capital cities (executive, legislative, judicial).
Recognises twelve official languages.
capital: Executive capital and seat of the President.
Known for its jacaranda-lined streets.
Cape Town is the legislative capital; Bloemfontein is judicial.
river: Longest river in South Africa (~2,200 km).
Rises in Lesotho and empties into the Atlantic Ocean.
Forms part of the border with Namibia.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

country: Smallest continent and one of the largest countries.
Federal parliamentary constitutional monarchy.
Primary de facto language is English.
capital: Purpose-built capital inaugurated in 1927.
Located inland, between Sydney and Melbourne.
Seat of the Australian Parliament and High Court.
river: Longest river in Australia (~2,508 km).
Forms much of the border between Victoria and New South Wales.
Central to the Murray–Darling river basin, the country's most important agricultural system.

provenance: CIA World Factbook; UN FAO Aquastat

label_variants.language_note: New Zealand has two de jure official languages (English and Māori); Aotearoa is the widely used Māori name for the country.

country: Island country in the southwestern Pacific.
Parliamentary constitutional monarchy.
Official languages are English, Māori, and NZ Sign Language.
capital: Southernmost capital city of any sovereign state.
Seat of the New Zealand Parliament since 1865.
Known for its windy weather and harbour setting.
river: Longest river in New Zealand (~425 km).
Flows from Lake Taupo to the Tasman Sea.
Has deep cultural significance for the Māori people.

provenance: UN General Assembly Resolution 67/19; Palestinian Authority government portal; Britannica

label_variants.language_note: Arabic is the official language. East Jerusalem is the capital declared by the State of Palestine; Ramallah is the de facto administrative seat.

country: UN non-member observer state since 2012.
Claims the West Bank and Gaza Strip as its territory.
Recognised as a state by a large majority of UN members.
capital: Declared capital under the 1988 Palestinian declaration of independence.
Its status is a central issue in the Israeli–Palestinian conflict.
Ramallah hosts the seat of the Palestinian Authority.
river: Forms part of the eastern boundary of the West Bank.
Shared with Israel, Jordan, Syria, and Lebanon.
Empties into the Dead Sea, the lowest point on land.

provenance: Government Portal of the Republic of China (Taiwan); CIA World Factbook (entry: Taiwan)

label_variants.language_note: Mandarin Chinese is the official language; native form uses Han characters (traditional). Latin local_display uses standard Pinyin romanisation.

country: Island country in East Asia, off the southeast coast of China.
Officially styled the Republic of China (ROC).
Recognised as a state by a small and changing number of UN members.
capital: Largest city and the seat of Taiwan's government.
Home to Taipei 101, once the tallest building in the world.
Situated in the Taipei Basin in northern Taiwan.
river: Longest river in Taiwan (~187 km).
Rises in the central mountain range and empties into the Taiwan Strait.
Divides the island roughly into northern and southern halves.

provenance: Government of the Republic of Kosovo portal; CIA World Factbook (entry: Kosovo)

label_variants.language_note: Albanian and Serbian are official languages of Kosovo.

country: Landlocked country in the Western Balkans.
Declared independence from Serbia in 2008.
Recognised by roughly half of UN member states.
capital: Largest city and political centre of Kosovo.
Seat of the Assembly and the President.
Name spelled Priština in Serbian and Prishtina in Albanian.
river: Rises in the Accursed Mountains in western Kosovo.
Joins the Black Drin in Albania to form the Drin river.
Shared with Albania along its lower course.

provenance: UN list of Non-Self-Governing Territories; Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic declarations; Britannica

label_variants.language_note: Arabic (Hassaniya and Modern Standard) is the dominant language; Spanish retains limited official use. Western Sahara is disputed between Morocco (which administers most of the territory) and the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic.

country: Disputed territory on the northwest coast of Africa.
Claimed by Morocco and by the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic.
Listed by the UN as a Non-Self-Governing Territory.
capital: Largest city in Western Sahara, under Moroccan administration.
Declared capital by the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic.
Tifariti hosts administrative functions in the Free Zone.
river: Seasonal watercourse (wadi) flowing through El Aaiún.
Gave its name to the northern Saguia el-Hamra province under Spanish rule.
Typical of the region, it carries water only after rare rainfall.

provenance: Government portal of the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus; UN Cyprus mission documents; Britannica

label_variants.language_note: Turkish is the official language of the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus. The TRNC is recognised only by Turkey; the rest of the international community considers the territory part of the Republic of Cyprus.

country: Self-declared state on the northern third of Cyprus.
Recognised only by Turkey; considered occupied territory by the UN.
Separated from the Republic of Cyprus by the UN buffer zone.
capital: Northern half of the divided city of Nicosia.
Seat of the TRNC Assembly and President.
One of the last divided capital cities in the world.
river: Longest river on the island of Cyprus (~100 km).
Rises in the Kyrenia (Girne) mountains and flows through Nicosia.
Largely seasonal, with flow concentrated in the winter months.
